stem: What is the primary output of proton MR spectroscopy?

options.text: A projection of maximum vessel intensity
A map of only longitudinal recovery times
A radiograph based on x-ray attenuation
A spectrum of metabolite signal versus chemical shift

explanation: MRS separates resonance frequencies to identify biochemical peaks rather than primarily depicting anatomy. Peak position identifies compounds and peak area relates to relative concentration.

stem: Which metabolite is commonly interpreted as a marker of neuronal integrity in brain proton spectroscopy?

options.text: NAA
Choline
Creatine
Lactate

explanation: N-acetylaspartate is concentrated in neurons and axons, so reduced NAA can indicate neuronal loss or dysfunction. Choline reflects membrane turnover, creatine is often a reference, and lactate suggests anaerobic metabolism.

stem: What does an elevated choline peak most directly suggest?

options.text: Improved neuronal integrity
Increased membrane synthesis or turnover
Complete absence of cellular metabolism
Only macroscopic blood flow velocity

explanation: Choline-containing compounds participate in membrane metabolism and often rise with high cellular turnover. The finding is nonspecific but can support active tumor or demyelinating processes in context.

stem: Why is water suppression used in proton MR spectroscopy?

options.text: Water contains no hydrogen resonance
Suppressing water increases the main field strength
The water peak would otherwise overwhelm much smaller metabolite signals
Metabolites can be measured only after T1 becomes zero

explanation: Tissue water concentration is far greater than metabolite concentration, creating a dominant spectral peak. Selective suppression reveals the much smaller metabolite resonances.

stem: How does PRESS define a spectroscopy voxel?

options.text: One readout gradient selects the brightest image pixel
A maximum-intensity projection chooses the vessel center
A saturation band measures every voxel outside the anatomy
Three slice-selective RF pulses applied along orthogonal planes select their intersection

explanation: PRESS uses a 90-degree pulse followed by two 180-degree pulses with orthogonal slice selection. Signal is refocused only from the volume where all three selected slabs intersect.

stem: Why is careful shimming especially important for MR spectroscopy?

options.text: A uniform B0 field keeps metabolite peaks narrow and separable
It changes every metabolite into water
It eliminates the need for frequency encoding
It makes chemical shift independent of molecular environment

explanation: Field inhomogeneity broadens resonance lines and can merge nearby metabolite peaks. Good shimming improves spectral resolution and the reliability of peak measurements.
